title: danthonia radicans
intro: Danthonia radicans, also known as Poverty Grass or Dune Grass, is a perennial grass species native to the coastal regions of New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its bioactive compounds such as phenolic acids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Danthonia radicans include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Danthonia radicans is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
